pg105-p1-s2:
Some of us veered from extreme to extreme, ever hoping that one day
our loved ones would be themselves once more.
pg105-p1:
driven to maudlin sympathy, to bitter resentment. Some of us veered from extreme to extreme, ever hoping that one day
our loved ones would be themselves once more.
pg105-p2-s1:
Our loyalty and the desire that our husbands hold up their heads and be like other men have begotten all sorts of
predicaments.
pg105-p2-s2: We have been unselfish and self-sacrificing.
pg105-p2-s3:
We have told innumerable lies to protect our pride and our
husbands' reputations.
pg105-p2-s4: We have prayed, we have begged, we have been patient.
pg105-p2-s10:
We have had retaliatory love
affairs with other men.
pg105-p2:
Our loyalty and the desire that our husbands hold up their heads and be like other men have begotten all sorts of
predicaments. We have been unselfish and self-sacrificing. We have told innumerable lies to protect our pride and our
husbands' reputations. We have prayed, we have begged, we have been patient. We have struck out viciously. We have run
away. We have been hysterical. We have been terror stricken. We have sought sympathy. We have had retaliatory love
affairs with other men.
pg105-p3-s1: Our homes have been battle-grounds many an evening.
pg105-p3-s2: In the morning we have kissed and made up.
pg105-p3-s3:
Our friends have
counseled chucking the men and we have done so with finality, only to be back in a little while hoping, always hoping.
pg105-p3-s4: Our men have sworn great solemn oaths that they were through drinking forever.
pg105-p3-s5:
We have believed them when no one else
could or would.
pg105-p3-s6: Then, in days, weeks, or months, a fresh outburst.
pg105-p3:
Our homes have been battle-grounds many an evening. In the morning we have kissed and made up. Our friends have
counseled chucking the men and we have done so with finality, only to be back in a little while hoping, always hoping. Our men have sworn great solemn oaths that they were through drinking forever. We have believed them when no one else
could or would. Then, in days, weeks, or months, a fresh outburst.
pg105-p4-s1: We seldom had friends at our homes, never knowing how or when the men of the house would appear.
pg105-p4-s2:
We could make few
social engagements.
pg105-p4-s3: We came to live almost alone.
pg105-p4-s4:
When we were invited out, our husbands sneaked so many drinks that
they spoiled the occasion.
pg105-p4-s5: If, on the other hand, they took nothing, their self-pity made them killjoys.
pg105-p4:
We seldom had friends at our homes, never knowing how or when the men of the house would appear. We could make few
social engagements. We came to live almost alone. When we were invited out, our husbands sneaked so many drinks that
they spoiled the occasion. If, on the other hand, they took nothing, their self-pity made them killjoys.
pg105-p5-s2: Positions were always in jeopardy or gone.
pg105-p5: There was never financial security. Positions were always in jeopardy or gone. An armored car could
